text: Honestly, for me Psychology is first and foremost...

options.text: A way to fix what's broken in the mind
A tool to understand why others act so weird
A luxury hobby for people with too much free time
The science of building a better version of myself

text: Which psychological school of thought provides the most practical solutions for modern life stress?

options.text: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)
Psychoanalysis and deep childhood exploration
Existential therapy and finding meaning
Gestalt and living in the 'here and now'

text: With the 2026 rise of AI-therapists, how do you feel about discussing your traumas with a bot?

options.text: Great, bots are non-judgmental and available 24/7
Okay for small issues, but I need a human for the deep stuff
Terrible, it lacks true human empathy
Dangerous, I don't trust bots with my mental data

text: My Identity is built more on my...

options.text: Professional achievements and career
Cultural heritage and family roots
Personal struggles and what I've overcome
Creative hobbies and hidden passions

text: In professional environments, how much of your authentic self do you choose to reveal?

options.text: 100% — I am the same person everywhere
75% — I hide only the most personal details
50% — I have a clear 'work persona'
25% — I am strictly professional and transactional

text: As hyper-realistic digital avatars become standard in 2026, does your online appearance feel as real as your physical one?

options.text: Yes, my avatar is the 'real me' I choose to show
No, it's just a costume for the digital world
It's starting to blur into one single identity

text: To me, Ethics is essentially...

options.text: Doing the right thing even when no one is watching
Following the laws of society to avoid trouble
A set of old rules that don't always fit today

text: Which ethical framework do you apply most often when making difficult decisions?

options.text: Utilitarianism: Whatever brings the most good to most people
Deontology: Following strict duties and principles
Virtue Ethics: Acting as a 'good person' would act
Egoism: Prioritizing my own long-term interests

text: In light of the 2026 'Social Credit' debates, should people be ethically judged by their digital footprint?

options.text: Yes, what you do online shows your true character
No, people should have the right to a digital 'reset'
Only for extreme cases of harm or hate

text: Ambition feels more like...

options.text: A fire that keeps me warm and moving
A heavy weight I can't ever put down

A compass that points to my true potential

text: What is the primary driver of your professional ambition?

options.text: Financial independence and security
Status, recognition, and power

text: In the 2026 'Quiet Excellence' trend, is it better to be secretly successful than publicly ambitious?

options.text: Yes, true power doesn't need an audience
No, public ambition inspires others

text: Happiness for me is fundamentally about...

options.text: Peace of mind and zero drama
The thrill of new experiences and travel

text: How much does your external environment (housing, city, weather) influence your baseline happiness?

options.text: Significantly — I am a product of my surroundings

text: As 'Dopamine Detox' becomes a 2026 lifestyle, which 'simple pleasure' actually makes you happiest?

options.text: Physical books and no screens
Long walks in total silence

Face-to-face conversation with a friend

text: Mental Health to me feels like...

options.text: A garden that needs daily weeding

text: What is your primary method for managing acute anxiety or burnout?

options.text: Professional therapy or medication
Intense physical exercise and movement

text: With 2026 workplaces introducing 'Mental Health PTO', do you feel comfortable telling your boss you're taking a 'brain day'?

options.text: Yes, the stigma is finally dead
No, I'd still say I have a physical illness
Only if the company culture is very modern

text: Health is...

options.text: Being able to do whatever I want without pain

text: Which health metric do you track most obsessively?

options.text: Sleep quality and duration

options.text: Yes, personalized medicine is the future
No, that sounds like a marketing scam

text: Gym Life is...

options.text: My church — the place where I find focus

options.text: Maximum strength and muscle gain
Fat loss and aesthetic definition

options.text: Yes, I want maximum results in minimum time
No, the struggle and 'natural' pain is the point

options.text: A digital job interview with no pay

options.text: A grand gesture in the rain

options.text: Yes, logic builds a marriage; sparks burn out

options.text: My safety net and greatest strength

options.text: Yes, I feel more understood by my friends

options.text: Yes, we are terrified of stranger interaction

options.text: A casino where the house always wins

options.text: The most efficient way to lift people out of poverty
